title: Lesemia coccinea
intro: Lesemia coccinea, also known as Western stickseed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Polemoniaceae and is native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lesemia coccinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lesemia coccinea is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lesemia coccinea is a species of fern, and it belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. This plant is native to the Americas and is classified as a vascular plant. Lesemia coccinea is further classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, and it is a type of leptosporangiate fern. The plant's classification also includes being part of the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species within the plant kingdom.

The plant's extracts have also been used to create remedies for digestive problems and to reduce inflammation.
